debug_info.goal:
case swap

l1 l2 : List α
x y : α
l : List α
⊢ ∀ {idx1 : Fin (y :: x :: l).length} {idx2 : Fin (x :: y :: l).length},
    (y :: x :: l)[idx1] = (x :: y :: l)[idx2] → ((y :: x :: l).eraseIdx ↑idx1).Perm ((x :: y :: l).eraseIdx ↑idx2)

simp [List.get] at H ⊢
    <;>
    (try simp_all [List.perm_swap, List.perm_cons] <;>
    (try {
      cases idx1 <;> cases idx2 <;>
      simp_all [List.get, List.eraseP]
      <;>
      (try { contradiction }) <;>
      (try { simp_all [List.perm_swap, List.perm_cons] }) <;>

      <;>

simp_all [List.Perm]
    <;> try simp_all [List.Perm]

  exact h₁

simp
    have h₅ : m < (y :: x :: l).length := by simpa [h₄] using h₃
    have h₆ : n < (y :: x :: l).length := hn
    have h₇ : (y :: x :: l).get ⟨n, h₂⟩ = (x :: y :: l).get ⟨m, h₃⟩ := by
      simpa [Fin.ext_iff] using h₁
    -- Consider different cases for n and m
    have h₈ : n = 0 ∨ n = 1 ∨ n ≥ 2 := by omega
    have h₉ : m = 0 ∨ m = 1 ∨ m ≥ 2 := by omega
    rcases h₈ with (rfl | rfl | h₈) <;> rcases h₉ with (rfl | rfl | h₉) <;>
      simp_all (config := {decide := true}) [List.get, List.erase_get, Fin.ext_iff, list_get] <;>
      (try contradiction) <;>
      (try permutation) <;>
      (try simp_all [List.Perm]) <;>
      (try {
        cases h₈ <;> cases h₉ <;>
          simp_all [List.get, List.erase_get, Fin.ext_iff, list_get] <;>
          (try contradiction) <;>
          (try permutation) <;>
          (try simp_all [List.Perm]) <;>
          (try {
            simp_all [List.Perm]
            <;> try permutation
          })
      })

intro idx1 idx2 hxy
    have h : (y :: x :: l)[idx1] = (x :: y :: l)[idx2] → ((y :: x :: l).eraseIdx ↑idx1).Perm ((x :: y :: l).eraseIdx ↑idx2) := by
      intro h₁
      have h₂ : ((y :: x :: l).eraseIdx ↑idx1).Perm ((x :: y :: l).eraseIdx ↑idx2) := by
        -- We need to handle the case when the indices are 0, 1, or greater than 1.
        have h₃ : (y :: x :: l)[idx1] = (x :: y :: l)[idx2] := h₁
        -- Consider the possible positions of the indices
        have h₄ : ((y :: x :: l).eraseIdx ↑idx1).Perm ((x :: y :: l).eraseIdx ↑idx2) := by
          -- Use the fact that the indices are in the correct positions to apply the permutation
          fin_cases idx1 <;> fin_cases idx2 <;> simp_all [List.eraseIdx, List.Perm]
          <;>
          (try { contradiction }) <;>
          (try { simp_all [List.Perm] }) <;>
          (try { aesop }) <;>
          (try {
            simp_all [List.Perm]
            <;>
            (try {
              apply List.Perm.swap_pos3
              <;>
              simp_all [List.Perm]
            })
          })
        exact h₄
      exact h₂
    exact h

simp_all [List.eraseIdx, List.get, Nat.succ_eq_add_one, add_assoc]
            <;> (try contradiction) <;>

                  <;>

                })
            <;> (try
              {
                apply IH
                <;>
                  simp_all [List.get, Nat.succ_eq_add_one, add_assoc]
                <;>
                  (try contradiction)
              })
          exact h₁
